source.title: Effects of suvorexant on sleep architecture and power spectral profile in patients with insomnia: analysis of pooled phase 3 data

confidence_rationale: Pooled RCT PSG data from two large Phase 3 trials (n=1482), 3-month follow-up, objective PSG measurement. Directly demonstrates suvorexant increases REM% (average +3.9% at Night-1) with only minimal SWS/N3 reduction (≤0.8%) -- consistent with MECH-176 architecture preservation prediction. Delta power increase in non-REM supports maintained or enhanced slow-wave homeostasis. Reduced REM latency indicates preserved REM generation machinery. Industry sponsorship (Merck) is a source of potential bias but the PSG measurements are objective and pre-registered.
failure_signatures: The study shows small decreases in N3/SWS% (≤0.8%) suggesting suvorexant is not fully architecture-neutral on Phase 1; MECH-176 predicts SWS preservation, and the small SWS reduction (though much less than benzodiazepines) is a partial failure of the architecture-preserving prediction.

No direct comparison to benzodiazepines or Z-drugs in this study -- MECH-176 architecture preservation claim requires head-to-head comparison to confirm superiority, not just differences from placebo.
Participants are insomnia patients (not MCI or dementia risk population); effects in cognitively at-risk older adults may differ.
Industry-sponsored study (Merck, manufacturer of suvorexant); all authors are Merck employees.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Suvorexant (20/15mg and 40/30mg, non-elderly/elderly) increases time in all sleep stages compared to placebo. Expressed as %TST: N3/SWS decreases by ≤0.8%, REM increases by up to 3.9% (largest at Night-1, diminishing by Month-3). Delta power in non-REM increases 4-8% at Night-1. REM latency reduced by ~40-50 epochs at Night-1.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-176 predicts orexin antagonists preserve or improve SWS/REM ratio vs GABAergic alternatives by removing wake-promoting drive without compressing phase machinery. The REM increase (+3.9%) directly supports Phase 4 preservation. The minimal SWS reduction (≤0.8%) is small compared to GABAergic agents (which typically reduce SWS by 10-20%) and the delta power increase suggests SWS homeostatic quality is maintained even if absolute stage time is minimally reduced.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The small SWS% decrease challenges the strong form of MECH-176 (full architecture preservation). The correct interpretation is comparative: suvorexant preserves architecture substantially better than GABAergic agents, not that it has zero effect on architecture. The 3.9% REM increase is directly consistent with MECH-176 Phase 4 preservation.
mapping.source_context: Pooled Phase 3 RCT data; adults with insomnia (mean age ~40s-50s, not elderly); PSG recorded at Night-1, Month-1, Month-3; n=1482.

confidence_components.notes: High mapping fidelity for REM preservation component. Transfer risk elevated: insomnia patients, not MCI/elderly risk population; industry sponsorship.
